tasks.description: Gate full-screen mode on timeline param. When true: (a) LayoutMainContent skips GlobalHeader, removes `container mx-auto` and containerPadding/containerSpacing from `<main>`, zeros all pane offsets in contentStyle; (b) Layout.tsx hides SocialIcons footer; (c) Verify EditorPaneTab.tsx:66-68 null-return covers the dots. Key files: `src/app/Layout.tsx`, `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx`, `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx`.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `useVideoEditorRouteState` and used it in `src/app/Layout.tsx` and `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx` so full-screen chrome suppression only activates on `/tools/video-editor` when `?timeline=` is present. `Layout` now disables the split-view wrapper and hides `SocialIcons` in shell mode; `LayoutMainContent` suppresses `GlobalHeader`, `GlobalProcessingWarning`, container padding, pane offsets, and the shared header slot only in shell mode. `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx` now hides the pane controls for any video-editor route via the shared route helper.

tasks.description: Extend drag protocol with variant data. In `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts`: add `variantId?: string` and `variantType?: string` to `GenerationDropData`. In `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx` handleDragStart (~line 261-277): enrich drag data with primary variant info. The executor MUST investigate the actual data shape: (a) log `image` at drag time to see available fields, (b) check if `image.id` IS the variant ID or the generation ID, (c) check React Query cache for generation_variants data, (d) if no variant ID available, either add it to the generation query in `useProjectGenerations.ts` or look it up from the query cache at drag time. The generation's displayed URL (`image.url`) IS likely the primary variant's URL and can be used directly.
tasks.executor_notes: Resolved the open variant-ID source by changing `src/shared/hooks/projects/useProjectGenerations.ts` to select `generations.primary_variant_id` plus the joined `primary_variant` location/thumbnail, then surfaced that through `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts` and `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/types.ts`. `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx` now drags the primary variant ID and a media-kind flag in `GenerationDropData`, and the dragged URL is the primary variant location rather than the thumbnail path.

tasks.description: Extend AssetRegistryEntry and add registerAsset to DataProvider. In `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts`: add `generationId?: string` and `variantId?: string` to `AssetRegistryEntry`. In `src/tools/video-editor/data/DataProvider.ts`: add `registerAsset(timelineId: string, assetId: string, entry: AssetRegistryEntry): Promise<void>` to interface. In `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts`: implement `registerAsset` by extracting the asset_registry column update logic from `uploadAsset` (lines 110-125) — load current registry, merge new entry, update `asset_registry` column. Refactor `uploadAsset` to call `registerAsset` internally after file upload.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended `AssetRegistryEntry` with `generationId` and `variantId` in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts`, added `registerAsset` to `src/tools/video-editor/data/DataProvider.ts`, and implemented it in `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts` as a read-merge-write of the `asset_registry` JSONB column. `uploadAsset()` now generates IDs with the shared UUID fallback and delegates the registry write to `registerAsset` so uploads still persist end-to-end.

tasks.description: Handle generation drops in timeline. In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts`: (a) Extend `onTimelineDragOver` (~line 461) to accept `application/x-generation` MIME type. (b) Extend `onTimelineDrop` (~line 530) with a new case: import `getGenerationDropData` from `dragDrop.ts`, create AssetRegistryEntry with `file: data.imageUrl`, inferred type, generationId, variantId. Generate assetId via `crypto.randomUUID()`. Call `patchRegistry` for local state AND `dataProvider.registerAsset` for DB persistence (fire-and-forget with error logging). Then call `handleAssetDrop` to create the clip. (c) Ensure the data provider is accessible — thread it through `useVideoEditorRuntime()` or as a dependency.
tasks.executor_notes: Added a provider-backed `registerAsset` wrapper in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts`, threaded it through `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts`, and extended `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts` so timeline dragover/drop now handles `application/x-generation` alongside files and asset-key drags. Generation drops create a local registry entry with `generateUUID()`, persist it through `registerAsset`, and then reuse `handleAssetDrop` to create the clip without disturbing file upload or asset-key paths.

On drop: same registration logic as T6 (create entry, patchRegistry, registerAsset) but WITHOUT creating a clip. Pass registration functions as props from PropertiesPanel or via context.

watch_items: CRITICAL: GenerationDropData variant ID sourcing (flag: generations-pane-not-variant-level). The `image` prop in MediaGalleryItem does NOT currently carry variant_id. The executor must investigate the actual data shape at drag time and find or add the variant ID. Options: (a) add primary_variant_id to the generation query in useProjectGenerations.ts, (b) look up from generation_variants via React Query cache, (c) if `image.id` turns out to BE the variant ID (check the DB schema), use it directly. This is the biggest unknown in the plan.

Verify that `image.url` in MediaGalleryItem is a direct Supabase storage URL usable as an asset file reference, not a thumbnail or transformed URL. If it's a thumbnail, find the full-resolution variant URL instead.

The `patchRegistry` function (useTimelineData.ts:339-390) only updates local state with `save: false`. The new `registerAsset` method handles DB persistence separately. Both must be called on drop — don't assume patchRegistry persists.

SupabaseDataProvider.uploadAsset() (lines 87-131) currently handles both file upload AND registry column update. When refactoring to extract registerAsset, ensure uploadAsset still works end-to-end by calling registerAsset internally.

EditorPaneTab.tsx:66-68 reportedly returns null on /tools/video-editor, but user reports dots still show. Investigate whether the dots render through a separate component path not covered by that null return.
The full-screen gating must check BOTH pathname AND searchParams.has('timeline'). The timeline list view at /tools/video-editor (no ?timeline=) must keep normal layout with GlobalHeader and footer.
crypto.randomUUID() may not be available in insecure contexts (HTTP). The codebase already has a fallback at src/shared/lib/ — check recent commit f20db198c for the pattern and reuse it.
When zeroing pane offsets in LayoutMainContent for full-screen mode, ensure this doesn't break the GenerationsPane positioning since users need it open to drag from.
MediaLightbox z-index and portal behavior — verify it renders correctly when opened from within the video editor component tree, which is itself full-screen with overflow-hidden.

meta_commentary:
**Execution order:** T1→T2→T3 (layout, serial). T4, T5, T9, T11 can run in parallel with each other and with T1-T3. T6 depends on T4+T5. T7 depends on T5+T6. T8 validates T6+T7. T10 depends on T5. T12 is final gate after everything.

**Biggest risk:** T4 (variant ID sourcing). The plan acknowledges this gap. The executor should start by reading the actual `GeneratedImageWithMetadata` type and logging the `image` prop to see what's available. Three likely outcomes: (a) `image.id` is already the variant ID (if the query selects from generation_variants), (b) variant_id exists in metadata, (c) neither — requiring a query addition or cache lookup. Option (c) is most work but still small scope.

**Parallel opportunities:** T1-T3 (layout) and T4-T5 (types/protocol) have zero overlap and can be done by separate agents. T9 (one-liner) and T11 (grep-and-replace) are independent of everything except T12.

**Don't over-scope T11:** The aesthetic polish should be mechanical find-and-replace of hardcoded colors. Don't redesign component layouts or add new visual features.

**The patchRegistry + registerAsset dual-call pattern:** Every drop must call BOTH. patchRegistry updates React state immediately (optimistic). registerAsset persists to Supabase. If registerAsset fails, the local state will be correct but next reload will lose the asset. Consider whether to add error handling beyond logging — for v1, logging is probably fine.

**GenerationsPane pane offsets:** When the editor is full-screen, pane margins are zeroed. But the user needs GenerationsPane open to drag from it. The GenerationsPane likely renders as a floating/overlay panel via PanesContext, not affected by main content margins. Verify this — if zeroing margins breaks GenerationsPane positioning, the pane offset suppression needs to be more surgical (only suppress left/right content margins, not the pane positioning system).
